rag_rules: Insert AGREE annexes only into implementation scopes when binding to subcomponent ports or connections defined in the AADL model.
generic_rules: Preserve AADL component hierarchy by anchoring AGREE annexes to the correct scope: type for interface-level contracts, implementation for internal routing.
Only reference ports and connections explicitly defined in the AADL model; do not invent paths or assume visibility.
syntax_or_pattern_reminders: Use annex agree {** ... **}; for implementation-level bindings, and ensure all referenced features exist in the target implementation.
Do not place guarantee clauses in implementation scope unless they bind to declared outputs or auxiliary variables.
anti_patterns: Do not reference AADL properties (e.g., Period, Deadline) as AGREE variables; they are not in scope unless mapped to explicit ports or constants.
Do not encode event ports as Boolean or numeric values unless an explicit data value is provided in the model.
irrelevant_topics: AADL Property Names Are Not AGREE Variables
